key_insight: A "BOPPPS + Xuexitong" blended teaching model can make abstract mechanical-design content more engaging and understandable for higher vocational students.
engineering_relevance: The study demonstrates a practical course-design framework for improving learning outcomes in foundational mechanical engineering education through online-offline integration.
method: Combine BOPPPS stages with Xuexitong platform functions across pre-class, in-class, and after-class phases.
Use bridge-in, objectives, pre-assessment, participatory learning, post-assessment, and summary to structure teaching.
Apply the model to "Composition and Classification of Hinge Four-bar Mechanisms" as a case lesson.
evidence_signal: Teaching practice reported increased student interest, stronger classroom participation, and better understanding of abstract knowledge, indicating improved teaching quality.
watch_item: The abstract does not provide quantitative comparison data, so the effectiveness claim appears to be based on practice observation rather than detailed measured metrics.
paper_title: Research on the Application of “BOPPPS + Xuexitong” Blended Teaching in Mechanical Courses of Higher Vocational Colleges: Taking Fundamentals of Mechanical Design as an Example
